compressed_prompt:
Write an acoustic song about the Korean peninsula without using any commas.  
Compose an acoustic song on the Korean peninsula theme no commas.
response:
# **Two Shores / One River**
*(Acoustic Folk Song)*

---

**[Verse 1]**
Across the mountains wrapped in morning mist
The Han River runs through every fist
Of stone and soil and memory so deep
A thousand years of stories in our sleep

**[Pre-Chorus]**
North and south beneath the same pale moon
The cranes still fly from June to June

**[Chorus]**
Oh peninsula reaching to the sea
Divided land still dreaming to be free
The pine trees grow on both sides of the wire
And grandmothers still tend the same old fire

**[Verse 2]**
The fishermen cast nets in yellow waves
While children learn the names of ancient graves
Pyongyang and Seoul both hear the morning bell
Two halves of one long story left to tell

**[Pre-Chorus]**
North and south beneath the same pale moon
The cranes still fly from June to June

**[Chorus]**
Oh peninsula reaching to the sea
Divided land still dreaming to be free
The pine trees grow on both sides of the wire
And grandmothers still tend the same old fire

**[Bridge]**
Seventy years of silence on the line
But roots grow deep beneath the DMZ vine
One language whispered through the mountain air
One people carrying more than they can bear

**[Outro]**
Oh carry me home across that frozen ground
Where north meets south and peace is finally found
Where north meets south and peace is finally found

---

*Tuning: DADGAD — Capo 3*
